questions.explanation: The present continuous is be + verb-ing, and with I the form of be is am: I am doing. "doing" alone has no be, "is doing" uses the wrong be for I, and "do" is the present simple (for habits, not for right now).

"Look!" tells us it is happening now, so use the present continuous. With it the form is is + -ing: it is raining. "are raining" uses the plural be wrongly, "raining" has no be, and "rains" is the present simple (a general habit).

questions.prompt: What is the correct -ing form of "run"?
questions.explanation: run has one short vowel + one consonant, so we double the n before -ing: running. "runing" forgets to double, "runnning" has too many n's, and "runeing" adds an extra e that does not belong.

questions.prompt: Complete: "She ___ a letter to her friend."
questions.explanation: With she the form of be is is, and the continuous keeps the -ing: she is writing. "are writing" uses the plural be, "am writing" is only for I, and "is write" drops the -ing the continuous needs.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We ___ to music in the garden."
questions.explanation: With we the form of be is are, plus the -ing form: we are listening. "is listening" is singular (he/she/it), "am listening" is only for I, and "listening" alone is missing the verb be.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct -ing form of "have" (as in eating) in "They ___ lunch now."?
questions.explanation: have ends in a silent -e, so we drop the e before -ing: having. With they the be is are: they are having lunch. "are haveing" keeps the e wrongly, "are have" forgets the -ing, and "having" alone has no be.
